scope: Break down what the forward-carried group-id sidecar adds inside the query-major sparse-MLA dKV producer

mode4_baseline_checksum.read: Baseline checksum path: K16 query-major producer loads route metadata inside each K16 tile and sinks dKV contribution.

mode17_sidecar_preload_unused_checksum.read: Adds a full selected-window sidecar preload into shared memory, then ignores it and runs the baseline mode-4 route path. This isolates duplicate sidecar preload traffic plus one extra synchronization.

mode3_sorted_sidecar_valid_dkv.read: Baseline valid-dKV path using the sorted group sidecar as the direct per-K16 route metadata.

mode18_sidecar_preload_unused_valid_dkv.read: Adds full selected-window sidecar preload, ignores it, then runs the baseline valid-dKV path. This isolates duplicate sidecar preload traffic with final dKV atomics present.

mode15_sidecar_used_checksum.read: Uses the preloaded sidecar in the K16 epilogue instead of rereading route metadata from global memory; checksum output.

mode16_sidecar_used_valid_dkv.read: Uses the preloaded sidecar in the K16 epilogue instead of rereading route metadata from global memory; valid dKV output.

what_is_added: For each token/selected-window CTA, read valid_u8[B,S,K] and safe_groups[B,S,K] once.
Store group_all_sh[K] and valid_all_sh[K] in LDS/shared memory.
Synchronize once before the normal K16 score/prob/scaled-dP loop.
When the sidecar is used, each K16 tile reads group_all_sh/valid_all_sh from LDS instead of loading safe_groups/valid_u8 from global memory.

duplicate_preload_global_bytes_mode17_or.read: Mode17/18 intentionally duplicate the metadata reads, so this is an upper-bound attribution for extra HBM sidecar traffic.

sidecar_shared_memory_bytes_when_used.read: Mode15/16 move the same global metadata reads into a sidecar preload and add this LDS/shared-memory store/read footprint. The global route traffic itself is not meaningfully larger than mode3/4.

dominant_producer_traffic_estimate.read: These are shape-level lower-bound traffic estimates from the current one-token K16 producer. They dwarf the sidecar metadata traffic and explain why the sidecar preload is not visible as a meaningful latency delta.

decision.read: The sidecar metadata path adds a small amount of HBM/LDS traffic, but measured attribution says it is not the producer wall: duplicate sidecar preload is within noise of the baseline, and using the sidecar instead of direct route reads is also neutral. The remaining latency is the producer/epilogue itself: repeated q and grad_output reloads across selected K tiles, replay of prob/scaled-dP from shared memory across D blocks, and H-wave contribution reduction traffic before dKV emission.
decision.next: Keep the forward sidecar as the correct metadata contract, but target the producer memory schedule: reduce q/dO reload replay and make the dKV epilogue group-aware without staging/replaying full prob/scaled-dP or H-wave contribution slabs.
